Risk Desc: As new developments and releases are introduced in Schedule X, there is a possibility that the Upgrade environments may not be updated in a timely manner, leading to inconsistencies and the environments being out of sync. Project-Level           External                     Environmental      Moderate      Likely Very High
Category:
Establish a regular sync process to ensure that all new developments and releases in Schedule X are reflected in the Upgrade environments.
Automate environment refresh or synchronization wherever possible to reduce manual effort and oversight.
Assign ownership to a dedicated release manager or DevOps resource to track and validate updates across environments.
Include an environment sync checkpoint in the release checklist to confirm alignment before testing or migrations begin.

This may result in missed defects, inaccurate upgrade readiness validation, and potential delays in migration timelines. Risk Title: 4 Integration recipes will not be available for end-to-end testing during pilot: Census IN, Schedule Out, PTO Balance IN/Show, Approved PTO IN
Risk Desc:
We will not have below 4 integration recipes developed and available for testing during the pilot phase
Census IN, Schedule Out, PTO Balance IN/Show, Approved PTO IN

end-to-end testing of migration of data with these recipes will not be possible. Project-Level           External                     Environmental      Moderate Very Likely Very High The approach we're taking means that we can add new integrations quickly as soon as they become available with minor to no change to the routines performing the upgrade. What should be in scope for the pilot is the following:

- collection of the api or sftp connection info for every integrations regardless of type, part of e2e for the ones in the pilot
- routine/data pipeline that handles updating configuration info in workato for connectors, this should be general purpose and work for any sftp or api, e2e for the pilot

- collection of the job run times from all existing integrations regardless of type, part of e2e for the ones in the pilot
- routine/data pipeline that handles updating configuration info in workato for file or api triggers related to when the job should run, this should be general purpose and work for any sftp or api, e2e for the pilot

- collection of the custom config and mappings of all existing integrations regardless of type, part of e2e for the ones in the pilot
- routine/data pipeline that handles updating configuration info in workato lookup table, this should be general purpose and work for any sftp or api, e2e for the pilot
